description: MCP typed-tool surface catalog (#284). Declares the substrate operations a Model Context Protocol server exposes to Claude + other AI tooling. The catalog is the canonical source of truth: MCP server impl reads this file; substrate gates can refuse calls to tools not in the catalog.

tools.name: Run forge build
tools.description: Execute the full Forge build pipeline. Walks every phase from validate_cms through the variation gates and renders + audits the site. Equivalent to `forge build` from the CLI.

tools.name: Show current site identity
tools.description: Compute the current SHA-256 hash of the [site_identity] section of forge.toml and surface key fields (site_id, tenant_id, voice tier, mood primary, density, theme variants, allowed/forbidden primitives).

tools.name: List identity provenance history
tools.description: Enumerate reports/provenance-*.json entries sorted by timestamp; each entry carries the identity_hash + fingerprint commitment + Ed25519 signature.

tools.name: Verify provenance signature
tools.description: Verify an Ed25519 signature on a provenance file against reports/attest-pubkey.b64. Returns ok / fail; on fail, includes the verification error.

tools.name: Verify fingerprint registry chain
tools.description: Walk the Merkle-chained fingerprint registry, validating each entry's hash + prev_hash + (optional) Ed25519 signatures.

tools.name: List fingerprint registry entries
tools.description: Enumerate registry entries; supports tenant filter.

tools.name: Initialize attestation keypair
tools.description: Generate an Ed25519 keypair and write to reports/attest-key.b64 (private, mode 0600) + reports/attest-pubkey.b64 (public). Refuses to overwrite without force=true.

tools.name: Read attestation public key
tools.description: Return the base64 public key from reports/attest-pubkey.b64 for external auditor pinning.

tools.name: Query AVP-Doctrine rule
tools.description: Look up a doctrine rule by id (e.g. `prim-001`) and return statement + rationale + enforcement contract.

tools.name: Render CMS to HTML
tools.description: Invoke Loom's cms-render against one cms/*.json file or the whole directory; emits static/<slug>.html via the canonical page_shell.

tools.name: Capture reference site
tools.description: Drive Crawler to fetch + screenshot + structure a reference URL at the declared viewports. Output feeds the reference-matching arc (#263-#274).

tools.name: Invoke a skill
tools.description: Run a substrate skill by slug (e.g. `add-loom-primitive`, `variation-resolution`). Records the invocation via skill_telemetry (#278).

tools.name: Recent skill invocations
tools.description: Read the last N entries from reports/skill-telemetry.jsonl; optionally filter by skill_slug.

tools.name: Load structured context for session
tools.description: Aggregate site_identity + recent provenance + recent skill telemetry + ARCHITECTURAL_THREADS into a single context payload for fast session orientation. Cached per-session (#285).

tools.name: Substrate state machine snapshot
tools.description: Query the substrate's current state machine (#286): which arcs are in-progress, which gates are enabled, which content types are declared, which deprecations are pending.

tools.name: Emit structured progress event
tools.description: Append a structured progress event (#288) — used by long-running operations to surface intermediate state to the operator without freezing on a single tool result.

notes: Categories: substrate (build / render), introspection (read-only), attestation (signing / verification), reference-matching (Arc 2), automation (skills + progress).
Every tool that mutates filesystem state (read_only=false) MUST emit skill_telemetry records via skills.invoke when invoked by Claude — establishes the audit trail per #278.
requires_signed_session=true reserves the most sensitive operations (attest.init, future identity-rollback) for sessions that have explicitly authenticated with the operator key.
Per [[backward-compat-version-discipline]]: tool slug + inputs/outputs schema is the wire shape. Renaming a tool breaks consumers; deprecate via removal from this catalog with a 1-version overlap.
Future MCP server impl walks this catalog at startup to register tools. The catalog itself is the canonical contract; both the impl and the consumers (Claude) read from it.
